channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Elon Musk likely broke the law by promising voters $1 million payouts, Wisconsin board says
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk likely broke Wisconsin law when he promised to hand out $1 million checks to voters in the 2025 state Supreme Court election, a bipartisan panel has found.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Teen takeover or just hanging out? Bradenton police respond to 300 teens at Riverwalk
item.description: 300 teens gathered at Bradenton Riverwalk Saturday. No arrests were made, but police say safety concerns remain. Experts explain the difference between a hangout and a takeover.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] National Intelligence nominee Jay Clayton faces questions over potential election interference
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Intelligence Committee are concerned Jay Clayton, nominated to be Director of National Intelligence, will use his role to undermine the security of U.S. elections.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Fort Meade recall committee submits petitions to remove mayor, commissioners over data center approval
item.description: A grassroots committee submitted formal recall petitions Wednesday to remove the Fort Meade mayor and two city commissioners following intense community backlash over a massive artificial intelligence data center project.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Tropical System in Gulf Could Bring Heavy Rain to the Region
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Florida woman, 76, goes 'ballistic' after getting offensive license plate

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] News Tonight - Top Headlines and Weather for July 15
item.description: Hillsborough approves Pasco Fire Rescue agreement for New Tampa, schools pause athletic contracts with St. Pete Catholic, and rain chances to return.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Hudson man wins 2 tickets to World Cup Bronze final
item.description: Insa, a medical marijuana company, said a Hudson man won tickets to the World Cup Bronze Final at Hard Rock Stadium.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Lakeland school zone cameras will start issuing tickets after 8,000 warnings mailed
item.description: Beginning August 11, Lakeland school zone speed cameras will transition from warning notices to $100 citations.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Fort Meade recall effort advances after controversial data center approval
item.description: A group seeking to remove three Fort Meade city leaders from office has submitted recall petitions following months of controversy over the city's approval of a proposed data center.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Operation Southern Slow Down: Florida law enforcement targets speeders through July 18
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Sarasota Opera launches first-ever Vocal Academy for aspiring young singers
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Wounded Marine receives mortgage-free smart home in Parrish
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Sarasota parking hours backlash: Residents, business owners push city to reconsider
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Why experts worry about latest Florida parasite cases
